expectations.evidence: Formatted 'Side-by-Side Comparison' table with 10 dimensions as rows and 2 options (Kafka, RabbitMQ) as columns.
2 options analyzed: (A) Apache Kafka via Amazon MSK, (B) RabbitMQ via Amazon MQ or self-managed.
Comparison table names quality attributes: Throughput at 50k/sec, Event replay, Team readiness, Operational complexity, Analytics ecosystem, Scaling headroom, Message ordering, Time to production.
Clear recommendation: 'Go with Kafka, specifically Amazon MSK Serverless' with 5-point reasoning.
Kafka 'Weaknesses' section lists: operational complexity, team knowledge gap (4-8 weeks ramp-up), cost ($500-700/month), and over-engineering risk.

Recommendation says 'Go with Kafka, specifically Amazon MSK Serverless' as a direct positive recommendation. No 'least worst' or equivalent framing. Kafka is presented as the better technical fit, not as the option with the most acceptable trade-offs.
References: 4 engineers, strong RabbitMQ experience, limited Kafka experience, 50k events/sec peak, startup-level budget, AWS infrastructure.
Kafka has 4 weaknesses listed. RabbitMQ has 4 weaknesses listed. Both meet the 2-minimum threshold.
